decks.notes: The main concept of this Mono Black Aggro Legacy deck is to disrupt the opponent's hand with cards like Thoughtseize and Dauthi Voidwalker, while also using efficient removal spells like Fatal Push and Snuff Out to control the board. This deck aims to apply pressure with aggressive creatures like Nethergoyf and Barrowgoyf, while utilizing cards such as Dark Ritual and Moonshadow to quickly accelerate its game plan. The deck's land base includes fetch lands and Wasteland to ensure consistent mana access and disrupt the opponent's mana base.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan while applying aggressive pressure through creatures like Orcish Bowmasters and Murktide Regent. By utilizing cards like Brainstorm, Thoughtseize, and Force of Will, the deck aims to control the board and protect its threats. The inclusion of Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares provides additional card advantage and versatility. With a combination of removal spells like Fatal Push and Wasteland alongside efficient creatures and disruptive spells, this Dimir Tempo deck aims to outmaneuver opponents and secure a quick victory.

The deck aims to quickly and efficiently cheat powerful creatures like Griselbrand or Archon of Cruelty into play from the graveyard using cards like Reanimate, Animate Dead, and Shallow Grave. It also disrupts the opponent's hand and board state with cards like Cabal Therapy, Thoughtseize, and Collective Brutality. By combining powerful reanimation spells with disruptive elements, the deck seeks to overwhelm the opponent and quickly close out the game with its high-impact creatures. The graveyard is used as a resource to execute a game-winning combo before the opponent has a chance to react effectively.

The deck revolves around quickly getting out powerful creatures, specifically Emrakul, the Aeons Torn, using cards like Show and Tell, Sneak Attack, and Omniscience. By combining these cards with cards that manipulate the top of the library like Brainstorm and Ponder, the deck aims to disrupt opponents while progressing its own game plan. With strong counter spells like Force of Will and Spell Pierce for protection, the deck is designed to set up explosive plays and win the game in a single turn.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly ramp up mana using Cloudpost and other mana-producing lands and artifacts, allowing the player to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures like Ulamog and Thought-Knot Seer. The deck also utilizes cards like Trinisphere and Chalice of the Void to disrupt the opponent's strategy, while Planar Nexus can provide additional turns to further establish board dominance. Overall, the key strategy of this deck is to accelerate mana production, disrupt the opponent's game plan, and overwhelm them with high-impact threats.

The deck focuses on disrupting opponents' strategies with hand disruption and removal spells while deploying threats that can close out the game quickly. By combining powerful creatures like Murktide Regent and Orcish Bowmasters with disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push, the deck aims to control the pace of the game and win before the opponent can stabilize. The inclusion of cards like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares provide additional card advantage and threats that can snowball the game in the player's favor.

The deck focuses on using fast mana sources like Chrome Mox and Simian Spirit Guide to quickly cast disruptive cards like Chalice of the Void and Blood Moon, which can shut down the opponent's strategy. It also includes powerful creatures like Magus of the Moon and Broadside Bombardiers to apply pressure and end the game quickly. The goal is to establish a board presence that the opponent cannot easily deal with, while quickly closing out the game with aggressive plays and burn spells like Sundering Eruption and Fury.

The focus of this deck is to quickly and efficiently fill up your graveyard with creatures to take advantage of powerful reanimation spells and win the game by either milling out your opponent with Undercity Informer and Thassa's Oracle, or overwhelming them with a horde of zombies from Bridge from Below. The deck utilizes a combination of fast mana acceleration like Lotus Petal and Simian Spirit Guide, along with disruptive cards like Thoughtseize and Pact of Negation to protect its game-winning combo. It is a high-risk, high-reward strategy that aims to win the game in a single explosive turn.

The main focus of this Eldrazi Aggro deck is to quickly play powerful Eldrazi creatures like Thought-Knot Seer and Reality Smasher to disrupt the opponent's game plan and apply pressure. The deck utilizes ramp cards like Eldrazi Temple and Lotus Petal to accelerate into its threats, while also using disruptive cards like Chalice of the Void to slow down the opponent. The inclusion of cards like Eye of Ugin and Cavern of Souls ensures that the Eldrazi spells can be cast efficiently, while cards like Wasteland help disrupt the opponent's mana base.

The deck focuses on quickly assembling the combo pieces needed to win the game in a single explosive turn. By using powerful mana acceleration with cards like Dark Ritual and Simian Spirit Guide, the deck aims to cast spells to fill the graveyard with creatures like Narcomoeba and Balustrade Spy. This then allows for a game-winning Dread Return target like Thassa's Oracle to be put into play, triggering its win condition to instantly win the game. The deck is reliant on careful sequencing and managing resources effectively to execute its combo efficiently.

This deck focuses on playing low-cost artifacts to quickly swarm the battlefield while utilizing cards like Emry, Lurker of the Loch and The One Ring to generate value and create explosive plays. The inclusion of combo pieces such as Paradox Engine and Aetherflux Reservoir allow for powerful win conditions, while disruptive cards like Sink into Stupor and Hexing Squelcher help control the opponent's game plan. By combining aggressive creatures and powerful artifacts, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and secure a swift victory.

This deck focuses on assembling a powerful artifact combo using cards like Mystic Forge and Urza's Saga. By utilizing cards like Voltaic Key and Manifold Key to untap key pieces, the player can generate infinite mana with Grim Monolith and Kozilek's Command to dig through their deck and set up a winning board state. Tezzeret, Cruel Captain and Karn, the Great Creator provide additional utility and disruption while The One Ring acts as a finisher. The deck's goal is to efficiently assemble the combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with a powerful artifact engine.

The main concept of this Mardu Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and disruptive spells. The deck focuses on a combination of creature aggression and disruption, utilizing powerful cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah, Amped Raptor, and Goblin Bombardment to apply pressure and control the board. Additionally, cards like Cabal Therapy and Thoughtseize allow for disruption of the opponent's game plan, giving the deck the upper hand in the early game. Overall, the deck aims to be fast, disruptive, and proactive in securing victory.

This combo deck aims to quickly mill through the entire deck and win the game by having no cards left in the library and using Thassa's Oracle to win on the spot. Using a combination of fast mana, creatures that can sacrifice themselves for advantage, free spells, and protection through Pact of Negation, the deck aims to execute its game plan as quickly and efficiently as possible. The graveyard manipulation and recursive spells help ensure the combo can be achieved consistently, making it a powerful and explosive deck in the Legacy format.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on a fast and aggressive strategy, aiming to overwhelm the opponent with early creature drops like Amped Raptor and Sand Scout. It also includes efficient removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and disruptive cards like Hexing Squelcher to maintain tempo. The deck utilizes powerful planeswalkers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Guide of Souls to provide additional value and close out games quickly. The inclusion of utility lands like Karakas and Wasteland adds flexibility and disruption to the deck's game plan.

The main objective of this deck is to quickly and efficiently get big creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn onto the battlefield using cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. By utilizing powerful combo plays, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with massive threats while also disrupting their game plan with counterspells like Force of Will and Flusterstorm. The deck also includes card draw and manipulation spells like Brainstorm and Ponder to ensure consistency in drawing the necessary combo pieces. Ultimately, the goal is to outpace and outvalue the opponent through explosive plays and powerful creatures.
